p1|IND.summary: Perception of national economic situation compared to one year ago (better or worse).

p2|IND.summary: Expectation of national economic situation improvement or deterioration in the next year.

p5|IND.summary: Frequency of visits to place of origin.

p8|IND.summary: Self-identified racial/ethnic category (white, mestizo, indigenous, or Black).

p10|IND.summary: Whether any family members speak or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_1|IND.summary: Whether respondent's mother speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_2|IND.summary: Whether respondent's father speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_3|IND.summary: Whether respondent's maternal grandmother speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_4|IND.summary: Whether respondent's maternal grandfather speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_5|IND.summary: Whether respondent's paternal grandmother speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_6|IND.summary: Whether respondent's paternal grandfather speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_7|IND.summary: Whether respondent's spouse speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_8|IND.summary: Whether respondent's mother-in-law speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p10_1_9|IND.summary: Whether respondent's father-in-law speaks or spoke an indigenous language.

p12_1|IND.summary: Belief about whether indigenous language speakers are considered indigenous.

p13a_1|IND.summary: Perceived greatest advantage of being indigenous in Mexico.

p14a_1|IND.summary: Perceived greatest disadvantage of being indigenous in Mexico.

p15|IND.summary: Opinion on whether life is more difficult for indigenous or non-indigenous people.

p16|IND.summary: Knowledge of approximate number of indigenous groups in Mexico.

p17|IND.summary: Knowledge of approximate number of indigenous language speakers in Mexico.

p18_1a_1|IND.summary: First mentioned indigenous group that respondent can recall.

p20|IND.summary: Belief about whether skin color influences treatment people receive.

p21|IND.summary: Perception of existence of racism in Mexico.

p22|IND.summary: Belief about whether indigenous customs are an obstacle to regional progress.

p23|IND.summary: Opinion on most appropriate government action for indigenous peoples.

p24_1|IND.summary: Agreement level that indigenous and non-indigenous people should be treated equally.

p25_1|IND.summary: Perception of whether denying employment to indigenous people is discriminatory.

p26_1|IND.summary: Positivity or negativity of family's image of indigenous people.

p27_1|IND.summary: First mentioned type of work where indigenous men are most frequently seen.

p28_1|IND.summary: First mentioned type of work where indigenous women are most frequently seen.

p29|IND.summary: Agreement with statement about indigenous peoples (specific statement not fully specified).

p30|IND.summary: Perception of whether indigenous human rights are respected in Mexico.

p31|IND.summary: Belief about whether most indigenous people can attend school.

p32|IND.summary: Perception of whether indigenous people have equal employment opportunities as non-indigenous people.

p33_1|IND.summary: Perception of how effectively white people assert their rights in Mexico.

p34_1|IND.summary: Belief about whether being indigenous is an obstacle to obtaining good employment.

p35_1|IND.summary: Belief about whether indigenous people can freely express themselves.

p36|IND.summary: Hiring preference between equally qualified indigenous and non-indigenous candidates.

p37_1|IND.summary: Willingness to hire an indigenous person as childcare provider.

p38_1|IND.summary: Belief about whether children of mixed indigenous-non-indigenous parentage would face discrimination.

p39_1|IND.summary: Whether respondent has or has had an indigenous friend.

p40_1|IND.summary: Willingness to allow indigenous people to live in respondent's home.

p41_1|IND.summary: Agreement level with government providing food/provisions to indigenous people.

p43|IND.summary: Perceived reasons for indigenous poverty in Mexico.

p44_1|IND.summary: Belief about whether indigenous community poverty is due to lack of work effort.

p45a_1|IND.summary: Perceived principal problem facing indigenous peoples.

p46|IND.summary: Opinion on indigenous self-determination versus equal electoral participation with non-indigenous citizens.

p47|IND.summary: Opinion on indigenous peoples' right to decide whether to sell ancestral lands to mining or extraction companies.

p48|IND.summary: Opinion on appropriate language for indigenous children's education.

p49|IND.summary: Agreement with providing translation services versus requiring Spanish for indigenous access to public services.

p50_1|IND.summary: Importance of Spanish language instruction for indigenous children.
